# Provenance Policy for Generated PDD Sections

**Version:** 0.1.0
**Scope:** All carbon credit content generated by the PDD drafting agent
**Applies to:** Verra VCS waste-to-energy Project Design Documents

---

## Purpose

This policy defines which sources may be cited in generated PDD sections, establishes the boundary between retrieval-augmented generation and free-form generation, and defines the human review requirements for each section type.

---

## Source Hierarchy

Sections may ONLY be sourced from the following sources, in order of preference:

### Tier 1 — Approved Corpus (Preferred)
- **Internal:** The verified corpus of 13 VCS WTE PDDs in `data/corpus/normalized/`
  - All sourced from Verra's public registry, owned by Aiden Roake (atr@allotropevc.com)
  - All are machine-readable PDFs with confirmed pypdf extractability
  - All scored IN_BUCKET under the `verra-wte-initial` bucket configuration
- **Use when:** Drafting BOILERPLATE, NARRATIVE, or METHODOLOGY_DEPENDENT sections where structural examples are needed

### Tier 2 — Official Methodology Documents
- ACM0022 v2.0 — "Alternative waste treatment processes (large-scale)"
- ACM0003 — "Partial substitution of fossil fuels in cement manufacture"
- AMS-III.AJ — "Recovered waste plastics"
- VMR0044 — "Biochar"
- **Use when:** Quantitative formulas, eligibility criteria checklists, parameter tables

### Tier 3 — Official Registry and Regulatory Sources
- Verra project registry (verra.org)
- UNFCCC CDM methodology library
- National environmental agencies for grid emission factors
- **Use when:** Citing project registration status, baseline emission factors, regulatory compliance

### Tier 4 — Project-Specific Evidence (User-Provided)
- Environmental Impact Assessments (EIAs)
- Stakeholder consultation records
- Laboratory analysis reports
- Weighbridge and metering records
- Fuel supply contracts
- Structured workbook rows and normalized snapshots derived from the provided Vietnam WTE spreadsheet
- **Use when:** Filling EVIDENCE_BASED and QUANTITATIVE sections with project-specific facts

### Synthetic Assumptions — Allowed Only With Disclosure
- Synthetic assumptions may be generated only to keep an internal draft workflow moving when Tier 1-4 evidence is missing.
- Every synthetic assumption must be captured in a machine-readable assumptions register with:
  - field path
  - value
  - source type
  - rationale
  - confidence
- Synthetic assumptions may not silently replace missing evidence in HIGH_REVIEW or CRITICAL sections.
- Synthetic assumptions must never introduce methodology deviations, double-counting boundaries, or unsupported quantitative formulas.

### PROHIBITED — No Free-Form Generation From
- Training data knowledge (LLM parametric memory)
- Generic internet sources not in the corpus
- Speculative or invented case studies
- Unverified industry statistics

---

## Section-Type Rules

| Content Class | May Generate Freely? | Source Requirement |
|---|---|---|
| BOILERPLATE | Yes (with adaptation) | Cite corpus example(s) by document name |
| FACTUAL | NO — user provides | User input only |
| EVIDENCE_BASED | NO — structured retrieval | Cite Tier 1 or Tier 4 evidence |
| METHODOLOGY_DEPENDENT | NO — retrieve only | Cite official methodology text |
| QUANTITATIVE | NO — formula + official data | Cite methodology formula + Tier 3 source |
| NARRATIVE | Structured retrieval only | Always cite retrieved source(s) |
| OPTIONAL | Yes (or skip) | Cite corpus example if used |

---

## HIGH_REVIEW and CRITICAL Sections

Sections with `review_sensitivity: HIGH` or `CRITICAL` require:
1. At least one corpus example cited by document name and section heading
2. Explicit human review gate before the PDD is considered draft-complete
3. All quantitative claims traceable to official sources

**CRITICAL sections** (additionality, baseline scenario):
- **Absolutely no free-form generation** — retrieval from Tier 1 or Tier 2 only
- Structural outline must be copied from corpus example with project-specific facts substituted
- Human expert sign-off required in addition to standard review gate
- If a required input to a HIGH_REVIEW or CRITICAL section comes from a synthetic assumption, the section must remain review-gated and the affected field must appear in the blocked review list.

---

## Citation Format

When citing a corpus source in a generated section, use the following format:

> [CORPUS: `{document_name}`, `{section_heading}`]

Example:
> The baseline scenario is modeled on the facility described in [CORPUS: `VCS_Soc Son_Project-Description`, `Section 3.4 Baseline Scenario`], which assumes the waste would have been disposed of in the Soc Son landfill.

When citing a methodology:
> Emissions factors are taken from [METHODOLOGY: `ACM0022 v2.0`, Section 4.1]

When citing official registry data:
> Project registration: [VERRA REGISTRY: `{project_id}`]

---

## Double-Counting Prevention

Per `rules/verra/wte_methodology_rules.yaml`, any section that addresses:
- Landfill diversion claims (Section 1.16, 3.4, 4.3)
- Fuel substitution claims (Section 1.16, 4.3)
- Renewable energy certificates (Section 1.15, 1.16)

Must explicitly reference the `double_counting_prevention` boundaries and include a credit ownership delineation statement. No generated content may imply that the same waste tonnage generates credits under two separate methodologies.

---

## Compliance Verification

Before any section is marked complete, the following must be verified:
1. All cited sources are real and retrievable
2. No section contains uncited quantitative claims
3. HIGH_REVIEW sections have at least one CORPUS or METHODOLOGY citation
4. CRITICAL sections have human expert sign-off recorded
5. Any synthetic assumption referenced by the run appears in the assumptions register and blocked review list when material

---

*Policy version 0.1.0 — derived from corpus analysis of 13 VCS WTE PDDs, Verra methodology ACM0022 and ACM0003, and internal POC workflow (VCM Document Generation POC, April 2026)*
